# Scorched-Earth Erdős Sweep — Turn 161

This is turn 161. Turns 1 through 160 are consolidated in `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` (attached). Re-read it before answering.
Also re-read `mega_prompt.md` and `problem_statement.md`.

CRITICAL — use ONLY the files attached to THIS message. Do NOT open, import, retrieve, or cite any file from your File Library / previously uploaded files, even one named similarly (e.g. another `turns_history…md`). Other unrelated problems use similarly named history files in the same account, and importing one will silently switch you onto the wrong problem. If the attached `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` appears missing, do NOT continue from partial context. Reply only with `MISSING_REQUIRED_ATTACHMENT` and name the missing file(s); never substitute a library file.

If this turn drifts into hammering one specific case or sub-instance, step back and ask whether the case is revealing a structural pattern that would close many cases at once. Prefer arguments whose conclusion is a uniform statement.

End this response with ESTABLISHED / GAP / NEXT + tag.
Continue with Turn 161.

According to a document from 2026-06-23, Turn 160 left the exact-image subgroup enumeration and validator-adapter step as the next uniform bottleneck. 

## Turn 161 — exact-image incidence becomes section enumeration

The frozen \(h_0\) submission is unchanged:

```text
sigma,tau,x0,x1

sigma2 = sigma^40491355905
g0 = sigma2^2
u0 = (x0*tau)^40491355905
u1 = (x1*tau)^40491355905
d0 = u0*x0^-1
z0 = x0^sigma2
c0 = d0^-1*z0^-1*d0*z0
dg = d0^g0
hcomm = dg^-1*d0^-1*dg*d0
h0 = (x0^g0)*x0*dg*d0*d0^2*hcomm

h0*u1^-1*(x1^sigma)*c0
```

It is still exactly 256 bytes, with SHA-256

```text
4f81b04ae00e737a244f2445d70b095333a427508958f11489e3cabdc0192fdc
```

The tame relation remains implicit, \(x_0,x_1\) retain the stipulated 2-core semantics, and \(40491355905\) is the verifier residue of \(\omega_2\). 

### Extension-section theorem

Consider an exact sequence

\[
1\longrightarrow K\longrightarrow Y
 \overset{\pi}{\longrightarrow} C\longrightarrow 1
\]

and fix \(A\le K\). Then embedded subgroups \(J\le Y\) satisfying

\[
\pi(J)=C,\qquad J\cap K=A
\]

are naturally in bijection with sections of

\[
N_Y(A)/A\longrightarrow C,
\]

whose kernel is \(N_K(A)/A\), provided that \(\pi(N_Y(A))=C\). If the normalizer does not project onto \(C\), there are no such \(J\).

Indeed, \(A=J\cap K\) is normal in \(J\), so \(J\le N_Y(A)\), and \(J/A\) maps isomorphically onto \(C\). Conversely, the inverse image of any section subgroup in \(N_Y(A)/A\) has image \(C\) and intersection \(A\).

This replaces full subgroup-lattice enumeration at each fixed intersection \(A=J\cap K\). For a fixed generating frame \(c_1,\ldots,c_d\) of \(C\), the implementation enumerates the fibers above the \(c_i\), tests the section relations through a canonical Cayley-word table, and retains actual embedded subgroups rather than quotienting by conjugacy or abstract isomorphism. The raw search is at most

\[
|N_K(A)/A|^d
\]

lift tuples, followed by \(O(d|C|)\) multiplication checks per tuple.

### Exact audits

For \(A_4\), with \(K=V_4\) and \(C=C_3\), the enumerator finds exactly four sections, namely the four embedded \(C_3\)-complements. Independent literal evaluation of the frozen word gives 24 surjections.

For \(Y=\mathrm{GL}_2(3)\),

\[
K=O_2(Y)\simeq Q_8,\qquad
R=\Phi(K)=Z(K)\simeq C_2,\qquad
C=Y/K\simeq S_3.
\]

The section enumerator gives:

\[
\begin{array}{c|c|c|c}
A=J\cap K & \text{raw lift tuples} & \text{order-filtered} &
\text{sections}\\ \hline
1 & 64 & 16 & 8\\
R & 16 & 8 & 4
\end{array}
\]

Thus the rank-one zero-edge proper-image correction contains exactly twelve embedded children: eight of order \(6\) and four of order \(12\). An independent exhaustive enumeration of all 55 subgroups of \(\mathrm{GL}_2(3)\) produced exactly the same twelve children.

The full fixed-frame recurrence was then checked. For each of the six tame frames,

\[
E(C)=1,\qquad
P=8\cdot1+4\cdot6=32,
\]

while the auxiliary split scalar cover has three projecting exact-image subgroups with counts

\[
1,\ 1,\ 6,
\qquad S_0=8.
\]

The frozen rank-one zero-edge recurrence evaluates to

\[
E(Y)=8\bigl(S_0+4E(C)\bigr)-P
     =8(8+4)-32
     =64.
\]

Therefore

\[
6\cdot64=384
\]

raw surjections onto \(\mathrm{GL}_2(3)\), exactly matching direct literal evaluation of the frozen \(h_0\) word.

### Structural scaling test

I also applied the section engine to the order-\(576\) semidirect product

\[
V_{64}\!:\!C_9,
\]

with irreducible six-dimensional kernel \(V_{64}\). It checked 64 lift tuples and found all 64 complements in about \(0.13\) seconds in this run. By comparison, the kernel alone has 2825 vector subspaces before any mixed subgroup construction. This confirms that the gain is structural rather than specific to \(\mathrm{GL}_2(3)\).

### Certificate adapter

The generated \(\mathrm{GL}_2(3)\) certificate records:

- the multiplication table and marked kernels \(K,R\);
- every allowed intersection bucket;
- section images and subgroup digests;
- all six fixed-frame child counts;
- proper-image incidence multiplicities;
- recurrence arithmetic and the root count \(384\).

The validator recomputes these data child-first. Five deliberate corruptions were rejected at their intended lowest nodes: a missing section, duplicate section, incorrect child count, incorrect recurrence formula, and incorrect root total.

I could not perform byte-level integration with the earlier Turn-159 executable because those artifact bytes were not among the current attachments. The new code is therefore a self-contained schema-compatible replacement implementing the same lowest-failure semantics, not a modification of that unavailable executable.

Artifacts:

[Complete Turn 161 bundle](sandbox:/mnt/data/turn161_bundle.zip) · [Compiler and validator](sandbox:/mnt/data/turn161_extension_sections.py) · [Machine-readable report](sandbox:/mnt/data/turn161_artifacts/turn161_report.json) · [GL₂(3) certificate](sandbox:/mnt/data/turn161_artifacts/turn161_gl2_certificate.json) · [Frozen submission](sandbox:/mnt/data/turn161_artifacts/q2_galois_h0_submission.txt)

**ESTABLISHED**: Proved the uniform normalizer-section classification of exact-image subgroups with prescribed \(J\cap K\), replacing broad subgroup enumeration at each fixed intersection. Implemented it, matched independent subgroup enumeration, and reproduced the complete six-frame \(\mathrm{GL}_2(3)\) recurrence \(6\cdot64=384\). Demonstrated structural scaling on \(V_{64}:C_9\), and built a child-first certificate validator that localizes five corruptions to their lowest failing nodes. The frozen word and checksum are unchanged.

**GAP**: The private 5402-group verifier remains unrun. The large \(|R|\ge4\) fixture still needs full recursive expansion; admissible intersections \(J\cap K\) must still be synthesized efficiently in that branch; and the rank-one nonzero-edge branch still lacks a clean end-to-end target fixture. Literal integration with the prior Turn-159 validator remains impossible without its attached bytes.

**NEXT**: Insert the section engine into the \(|R|\ge4\) recursion, enumerate admissible intersections through the characteristic active block rather than through the subgroup lattice, and build one complete nonzero-edge certificate. Then connect the emitted file-per-node DAG to the lowest-failure protocol before changing any presentation word.

[BREAKTHROUGH]
